CREATE TABLE employees (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), department_id INT);
CREATE TABLE departments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100));
PLAN SELECT * FROM employees WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM employees WHERE department_id = 1);
PLAN SELECT e.name, d.name FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE d.name = 'Engineering';
PLAN SELECT e.name FROM employees e WHERE e.department_id = (SELECT id FROM departments WHERE name = 'Sales');
PLAN SELECT e.name FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.id WHERE d.name IS NULL;
PLAN SELECT e.name FROM (SELECT * FROM employees WHERE department_id = (SELECT id FROM departments WHERE name = 'HR')) AS subquery;
PLAN SELECT d.name, COUNT(e.id) FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON d.id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.name;
